title: Residual Connections
summary: Identity shortcut: $X_{\text{out}} = X + \text{SubLayer}(X)$. Gradient highway (backward) + forward coupling (early params influence later blocks directly). Residual stream as shared communication bus.
key_insights: $X_{\text{out}} = X + \Delta X$ — model only learns the delta, not full representation from scratch
Gradient: $\partial X_{\text{out}}/\partial X$ includes identity $I$ — gradients never vanish completely
FORWARD COUPLING: early params influence later blocks directly (not through transformation chains)
What to pass through residual is a design choice: full $X$, gated, projected, or partial

derivations.title: Gradient flow through $N$ residual blocks

steps.name: Single block
steps.math: $\partial X_{\text{out}}/\partial X_{\text{in}} = I + J_{\text{sublayer}}$. If $\|J_{\text{sublayer}}\|$ is small, gradient $\approx I$.

steps.name: Chain through $N$ blocks
steps.math: $$\frac{\partial X_N}{\partial X_0} = \prod_i (I + J_i) = I + \sum_i J_i + \sum_{i<j} J_i J_j + \ldots$$

steps.name: Key: the identity term
steps.math: Expansion always includes $I$ as leading term. Even if all $J_i = 0$, gradient $= I$. Mathematical guarantee against vanishing gradients.

steps.name: Without residuals
steps.math: $\partial X_N/\partial X_0 = \prod_i J_i$. If each $\|J_i\| < 1$: $\|\partial X_N/\partial X_0\| < \|J\|^N \to 0$ exponentially.

derivations.consequence: Residuals convert multiplicative gradient chain (prone to vanishing) into additive one (always includes $I$). This is THE reason deep transformers train.

derivations.title: Forward coupling — the less-discussed benefit

steps.name: Without residuals
steps.math: $X_6 = f_6(f_5(\ldots f_1(X_0)))$. Block 6 sees $X_0$ only after 5 transformations.

steps.name: With residuals
steps.math: $X_6 = X_0 + \Delta_1 + \Delta_2 + \ldots + \Delta_6$. Block 6 sees $X_0$ DIRECTLY as additive component.

steps.name: Consequence
steps.math: When embedding params change ($X_0$ changes), block 6 feels it directly — not through a chain of nonlinear transformations.

derivations.consequence: Residuals create direct forward coupling between ALL layers. The 'residual stream' is a shared bus all blocks read from and write to.

derivations.title: Residual step size and stability

steps.name: The ratio
steps.math: $r = \|\text{SubLayer}(\text{LN}(X))\| \,/\, \|X\|$. How much each block changes the stream.

steps.name: Healthy range
steps.math: $r$ should be $O(1)$, typically $0.1$ to $1.0$. $r \gg 1$: sub-layer dominates. $r \ll 1$: sub-layer contributes nothing.

derivations.consequence: Monitor residual step size per block — key diagnostic for training health.

geometric_properties: Monitor $\|\Delta\| / \|X\|$ ratio per block — should be $O(1)$
Cosine similarity between adjacent deltas — orthogonal = independent info (good)
Residual stream norm $\|X\|$ should grow gradually, not exponentially
statistical_properties: $\|\Delta_{\text{attn}}\|$ vs $\|\Delta_{\text{FFN}}\|$ per block — should be roughly balanced
Contribution of each block to final output — removing block $k$ changes output significantly?
numerical_properties: Element-wise addition: $O(Bnd)$ ops, zero extra parameters
Gradient through residual: $O(1)$ cost via identity shortcut
open_questions: OQ1: In dual-encoder with cross-attention, should residual bypass cross-attention? Bypass = refinement. No bypass = full replacement.
